sources.notes: Count labeled trees whose degree-sum parity by color matches a mask.
Prufer-sequence and EGF solution with coefficient DP.

annotation.summary: Count labeled trees on colored vertices such that each color class has prescribed degree-sum parity.
annotation.constraints: n,m up to 1e4, so direct degree-sequence DP is too slow; the color-class contribution must be compressed.
annotation.core_idea: Use the Prufer sequence bijection: deg(v)-1 is the number of occurrences of v. For a color with cnt vertices, the parity constraint becomes a parity constraint on the number of positions in the Prufer sequence colored by that color. Its EGF is (e^{cnt x} +/- e^{-cnt x})/2. Multiplying all color EGFs leaves a sum of exponentials; a DP over coefficients of e^{jx} and final summation of j^{n-2} gives the answer.
annotation.complexity: O(nm) coefficient DP; possible divide-and-conquer NTT optimization is noted but not necessary for common implementations.

solution_variants.name: prufer-egf-dp
solution_variants.summary: Convert degree parity constraints to Prufer color-count parity and multiply color EGFs.

solution_variants.name: divide-conquer-ntt
solution_variants.summary: The same EGF product can be optimized by divide-and-conquer polynomial multiplication.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log^2 n) style with NTT

tags.evidence: The solution counts labeled trees through the Prufer sequence bijection.
The key reduction is deg(v)-1 equals the number of occurrences of v in the Prufer sequence.
tags.description: Use the Prufer sequence bijection to count or characterize labeled trees by degrees.
tags.created_reason: Prufer sequence is a specific, reusable labeled-tree counting technique.

tags.evidence: Color-class parity constraints are represented with exponential generating functions.
tags.description: Use exponential generating functions where labeled positions contribute divided by factorials.
tags.created_reason: EGF-based counting should be retrievable separately from broad combinatorics.

tags.evidence: After simplifying the EGFs, coefficients over possible exponential exponents are maintained by DP.
FFT/NTT is an optional optimization for multiplying many generating functions.
